description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with ornate, gold-accented engravings in a Celtic knot pattern on both blades. The blades are broad, symmetrical, and have a dark metallic finish with a sharp edge, while the central body of the axe is reinforced with decorative gold elements and blue gemstone-like studs. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped in a reddish-brown material, and features metallic bands and rivets for added grip and structural integrity. The pommel and the top of the handle are capped with metal, and there is a ring at the base for attachment or hanging. The overall design is both functional and ceremonial, suggesting a blend of utility and display. The craftsmanship and detailing indicate a high-quality, possibly fantasy-inspired weapon.
description: A double-headed battle axe with gold Celtic engravings, a dark metallic finish, and a reinforced, leather-wrapped handle with decorative metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing both ornate blades with gold Celtic patterns and the detailed central body with blue studs.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blades and the pointed top and bottom caps of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the symmetrical blade engravings and handle details.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the cylindrical handle, metal bands, and the alignment of the double blades.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, straight handle wrapped in reddish-brown leather with metal bands and a ring at the base; appears ergonomic and grippy.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical blades with wide cutting edges, gold Celtic knot engravings, and a reinforced central body; no beard, with decorative studs.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and riveted mounting with visible metal bands and decorative fasteners.
